summary: AIR-DROW Final Release + Font Restoration: restores the local Kurdish typography asset, validates the delivery path and prepares a final on-device release candidate.
features: Restores the local Kurdish UI typography asset to the source and generated application path.
Adds source and generated-output verification for the local font, preload route, font-face rule and font response header.
Keeps previous hand reliability, safe export, Settings persistence, WebKit fallback and device-check features unchanged.
Prepares the final release candidate without claiming that physical Android/iPhone testing has already been performed.

privacy.drawings: Your drawing data and preferences stay on your device unless you explicitly export, share, or use an optional AI creation action.
privacy.camera: Camera permission is requested only after you choose to open the camera. Camera video is not saved by AIR-DROW.
privacy.handTracking: The deployed app uses the local same-origin hand model and runtime. It does not download a hand model while you draw.
privacy.checks: Readiness, Feature Check and diagnostics read only browser/app capability. They do not open a camera, trigger a download, upload a report, modify an artwork, or include camera frames, hand landmarks, drawing points, project titles or API URLs.

buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads the hand runtime and model from the same AIR-DROW site.
buildPolicy.delivery: The update package preserves the existing local Kurdish UI font during Termux replacement; the build validates source and local hand assets before publishing.

delivery.sourceFont: The Kurdish UI typography asset is included in the source application path and copied to public during build.
delivery.fontHeaders: The font is served same-origin with an explicit font/ttf content type and on-demand cache policy.
delivery.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads local same-origin application assets. Camera and hand model behavior remains user initiated.
delivery.finalGate: Run the on-device checklist on Android and iPhone/Safari after deployment before publishing a GitHub release tag.
